extravasation_management.action: Monitor IV patency. Manage per standard irritant protocol.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Vitals every 15-30 minutes during infusion (high CRS risk).
stop_criteria: Severe hypotension or cardiac instability
Anaphylaxis, severe hypersensitivity, or uncontrolled rigors

nursing_assessment: Assess for delayed CRS symptoms
Monitor for opportunistic infections (CMV, PCP)
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Report fever, severe chills, or difficulty breathing immediately.

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: High Cytokine Release Syndrome (CRS) risk. Profound prolonged T-cell depletion.
mandatory_checks: Verify active infection prophylaxis (Bactrim/Acyclovir)
Confirm premedications are administered 30 mins prior

report_to_physician: Hypotension/Tachycardia
Signs of active infection

extravasation_management.action: Ensure capsules are swallowed whole; do not crush.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Daily assessment for fluid status and respiratory changes.
stop_criteria: Acute respiratory distress (suspected DS)
Unexplained sudden weight gain or edema

nursing_assessment: Assess lung sounds and daily weights strictly
Monitor for severe headache (pseudotumor cerebri)
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Take with food. Do not take vitamin A supplements.

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: High risk of Differentiation Syndrome (DS). Severe teratogenicity.
mandatory_checks: Check current WBC count (prophylactic steroids often ordered if >10k)
Verify negative pregnancy status

manage_at_bedside: Dry skin and lips (apply emollients heavily)
report_to_physician: Shortness of breath or unexplained fever (DS alert)
Severe, unremitting headache
Rapid weight gain > 1-2 kg/day

extravasation_management.action: Ensure free-flowing IV. Monitor for local inflammation.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Continuous tele/ECG monitoring if high risk; frequent vitals.
stop_criteria: QTc prolongation > 500 ms
Onset of arrhythmias or syncope

nursing_assessment: Monitor continuous cardiac rhythm and strict I/O
Assess for signs of Differentiation Syndrome (dyspnea, weight gain)
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Report palpitations, dizziness, or shortness of breath instantly.

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: High risk of fatal QTc interval prolongation and Differentiation Syndrome.
mandatory_checks: Verify baseline/recent ECG (QTc < 460 ms)
Ensure Potassium > 4.0 mEq/L and Magnesium > 2.0 mg/dL

report_to_physician: Syncope or irregular heartbeat
Shortness of breath with fever
Electrolytes dropping below target parameters

extravasation_management.action: Stop infusion. Do not flush violently if extravasation is suspected.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Vitals q15 mins for first hour. Direct observation for hypersensitivity.
stop_criteria: Signs of anaphylaxis (urticaria, wheezing, hypotension)
Sudden severe abdominal pain (pancreatitis alert)

nursing_assessment: Monitor amylase/lipase and blood glucose
Perform strict neuro checks (risk of cerebral sinus thrombosis)
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Report severe stomach pain, sudden headaches, or unusual bleeding.

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: High risk of severe coagulopathy (hemorrhage/thrombosis), pancreatitis, and anaphylaxis.
mandatory_checks: Verify Fibrinogen level is > 1.0 g/L (or > 100 mg/dL)
Ensure emergency anaphylaxis kit is at bedside

manage_at_bedside: Monitor hyperglycemia (administer insulin sliding scale as ordered)
report_to_physician: Severe abdominal pain radiating to back
Sudden neurological deficits or severe headache
Hypersensitivity reactions

extravasation_management.action: If SubQ, rotate sites. If IV, verify brisk blood return.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Standard vitals pre and post administration.
stop_criteria: Acute hypersensitivity (rare but possible)
Severe pain at IV insertion site

nursing_assessment: Assess injection sites for severe erythema or necrosis
Monitor bowel habits (high risk of constipation with prophylactic anti-emetics)
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Expect local redness at injection sites; apply cold compresses for comfort.

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: Drug is highly unstable once reconstituted (administer within 1-4 hours based on temperature).
mandatory_checks: Verify administration timing limits with pharmacy
Check current CBC for severe neutropenia/thrombocytopenia

report_to_physician: Fever > 38.0°C (neutropenic sepsis risk)
Bleeding or severe petechiae
Injection site showing signs of active infection/necrosis

extravasation_management.action: Stop immediately. Aspirate residual. Apply COLD compresses.
extravasation_management.antidote: Sodium Thiosulfate (if protocol specifies for severe cases)

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Observe closely for skin reactions/anaphylaxis during infusion.
stop_criteria: Loss of blood return or pain at IV site
Development of acute rash or respiratory distress

nursing_assessment: Monitor renal function, uric acid, and potassium (TLS watch)
Assess for progressive skin rashes (Stevens-Johnson risk)
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Maintain high oral fluid intake for 48 hours post-infusion.

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: Risk of severe Tumor Lysis Syndrome (TLS) and secondary viral infections (opportunistic).
mandatory_checks: Confirm active viral prophylaxis (Acyclovir/Valacyclovir)
Ensure aggressive hydration orders are active

report_to_physician: New onset peeling skin rash or mucosal lesions
Decreased urine output or flank pain
Signs of extravasation

extravasation_management.action: Ensure line is secure. Do not manipulate the pump aggressively.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Vitals q4h. ICE (neuro) score q4-8h minimum.
stop_criteria: Any change in baseline neuro status (tremors, confusion, aphasia)
Fever > 38.5°C accompanied by hypotension (CRS)

nursing_assessment: Maintain strict continuous neurologic evaluations
Monitor liver enzymes daily
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Do not manipulate the IV pump. Request help for any bathroom trips.

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: NEVER FLUSH THE IV LINE (fatal bolus risk). High risk of Cytokine Release Syndrome (CRS) and Neurotoxicity (ICANS).
mandatory_checks: Confirm Dexamethasone given strictly prior to first dose/step-ups
Verify dedicated continuous IV line without any other concurrent meds

manage_at_bedside: Low-grade fever (administer scheduled antipyretics and monitor)
Mild headache (if neuro assessment is otherwise stable)
report_to_physician: Deterioration in handwriting or ICE score (ICANS)
New onset tremors, seizures, or aphasia
Signs of severe CRS (Hypoxia, high fever, hypotension)

extravasation_management.action: SubQ route preferred to minimize vascular risk. If IV, ensure free flow.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Check vitals pre and post dose for orthostatic changes.
stop_criteria: Acute severe orthostatic hypotension drops
Severe acute worsening of neuropathic pain

nursing_assessment: Assess for autonomic neuropathy (bowel motility, orthostasis)
Monitor site for local reaction (SubQ common)
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Sit on edge of bed before standing. Report new numbness or tingling.

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: High risk of Herpes Zoster (Shingles) reactivation and severe peripheral neuropathy.
mandatory_checks: Confirm active antiviral prophylaxis is prescribed and taken
Perform baseline neuropathy assessment prior to every dose

manage_at_bedside: Mild orthostasis (encourage slow position changes and hydration)
report_to_physician: New or worsening burning, numbness, or pain in hands/feet
Severe dizziness upon standing (syncope risk)
Signs of shingles rash

extravasation_management.action: Stop infusion if pain occurs. Monitor for local phlebitis.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Vitals q1h during high-dose infusions. Strict I/O monitoring.
stop_criteria: Gross visible hematuria in Foley or urinal
Signs of acute heart failure (rapid weight gain, crackles, shortness of breath)

nursing_assessment: Mandatory strict I/O. Maintain high urine output.
Assess urine for blood (dipstick if suspected)
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Drink lots of fluids. You MUST wake up in the night to empty your bladder.

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: Severe hemorrhagic cystitis risk. Dose-dependent fatal cardiotoxicity (necrosis/CHF).
mandatory_checks: Verify robust hydration orders (Target 3L/day total fluid)
Confirm Mesna availability and dosing times (if high-dose protocol)

report_to_physician: Pink, red, or bloody urine
Dysuria (burning during urination)
Chest pain, rapid heartbeat, or sudden shortness of breath

extravasation_management.action: Verify IV patency continuously.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Continuous neuro checks per protocol. Check eyes for redness.
stop_criteria: Any change in cerebellar function (nystagmus, dysmetria, ataxia, slurred speech)
Acute severe eye pain

nursing_assessment: Monitor for 'Cytarabine Syndrome' (fever, maculopapular rash, bone pain) 6-12h post-dose
Administer scheduled eye drops faithfully (continue 24-48h post final dose)
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Report any blurriness of vision, feeling off-balance, or gritty feeling in eyes.

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: High-dose (HiDAC) causes severe irreversible cerebellar toxicity and chemical conjunctivitis.
mandatory_checks: Perform baseline cerebellar neuro check (signature/writing test) prior to every dose
Verify Dexamethasone eye drops are actively administered

manage_at_bedside: Administer eye drops precisely on schedule
report_to_physician: Worsening handwriting or inability to walk straight (Ataxia)
Severe eye burning or tearing despite drops
Fever with severe bone pain

extravasation_management.action: STOP IMMEDIATELY. Do not flush. Aspirate residual. Apply COLD PACKS.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Direct nurse presence required during entire push/infusion.
stop_criteria: ANY loss of blood return, resistance to push, or patient report of pain/burning
Sudden cardiac arrhythmias

nursing_assessment: Assess oral mucosa closely for severe mucositis
Monitor cardiac rhythm post-infusion
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Your urine will turn red/orange for 1-2 days. Use soft toothbrush.

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: CRITICAL VESICANT. Deep tissue necrosis on extravasation. Lifetime cumulative cardiotoxicity.
mandatory_checks: Verify baseline ECHO/MUGA LVEF is acceptable
Verify central line patency with brisk, flawless blood return

manage_at_bedside: Provide aggressive ice chips (cryotherapy) during infusion to prevent mucositis
report_to_physician: Signs of extravasation (swelling, redness, pain at site)
Palpitations, sudden edema, or shortness of breath (Cardiotoxicity)
Fever in presence of severe mucositis

extravasation_management.action: Standard irritant care. Ensure patent line.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Routine vitals. Observe for acute neuro changes.
stop_criteria: Sudden acute altered mental status, visual changes, or seizures
Severe hypersensitivity

nursing_assessment: Monitor for profound delayed myelosuppression and opportunistic infections
Assess for autoimmune hemolytic anemia (jaundice, sudden hemoglobin drop)
post_infusion_care.patient_education: You are at high risk for infection for months after this drug. Avoid sick contacts.

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: Severe T-cell depletion. Fatal Transfusion-Associated Graft-Versus-Host Disease (TA-GVHD) risk.
mandatory_checks: CRITICAL: All transfused blood products MUST be irradiated
Verify PJP (Bactrim) and Antiviral (Acyclovir) prophylaxis are active

manage_at_bedside: Ensure blood bank is alerted to "Irradiated Only" status
report_to_physician: Visual disturbances, severe confusion, or coma (Neurotoxicity)
Signs of anemia (extreme fatigue, pale skin, jaundice)
Any signs of infection or fever

extravasation_management.action: N/A

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Daily CBC monitoring during initial debulking phase.
stop_criteria: WBC drops below designated safety parameters rapidly
Severe TLS lab derangements

nursing_assessment: Assess lower extremities for painful, non-healing leg ulcers
Monitor renal function and uric acid
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Wash hands after handling. Do not open capsules. Use sun protection.

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: Severe bone marrow suppression. Extreme TLS risk upon rapid debulking of WBC.
mandatory_checks: Confirm hazardous drug handling precautions (wear chemo gloves for oral prep)
Verify Allopurinol/Hydration ordered if starting for massive leukocytosis

manage_at_bedside: Mild nausea (take with food if acceptable)
report_to_physician: Development of painful skin ulcers on legs/ankles
Rapid decrease in urine output
Signs of severe anemia or infection

extravasation_management.action: Stop infusion immediately. Aspirate. Apply WARM COMPRESSES ONLY.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Continuous direct observation and line assessment during administration.
stop_criteria: Patient complains of burning/pain at IV site
Any signs of redness, swelling, or loss of blood return

nursing_assessment: Assess for constipation and strict bowel sounds (paralytic ileus risk)
Check for severe neurotoxicity (foot drop, severe jaw pain, loss of deep tendon reflexes)
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Report numbness in fingers/toes, jaw pain, or lack of bowel movements immediately.

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: NEVER INTRATHECAL. Fatal if given spinally. Must be dispensed in a minibag to prevent accidental IV push errors.
mandatory_checks: Verify patient ID strictly
Confirm IV patency with brisk blood return (Potent Vesicant)
premeds: Laxatives (prophylactic bowel regimen to prevent fatal paralytic ileus)

report_to_physician: Signs of paralytic ileus (no bowel movement, severe bloating)
New onset foot drop or loss of motor function
Severe jaw pain

extravasation_management.action: Ensure tablets are swallowed whole; do not crush, cut, or chew.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Routine inpatient/outpatient assessment. Close monitoring of respiratory status and vital signs.
stop_criteria: Severe respiratory distress (suspected pleural effusion or PAH)
Grade 3 or 4 hemorrhage (GI, CNS, or other)
QTc prolongation > 500 msec

nursing_assessment: Assess respiratory status strictly for dyspnea, dry cough, or hypoxia
Monitor fluid balance and daily weights for signs of severe fluid retention
Assess for signs of gastrointestinal, CNS, or mucosal bleeding
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Swallow tablets whole. Do not take with grapefruit juice. Immediately report shortness of breath or sudden weight gain.

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: High risk for severe fluid retention (especially pleural/pericardial effusion), Pulmonary Arterial Hypertension (PAH), and severe myelosuppression.
mandatory_checks: Confirm hazardous drug handling precautions (wear chemo gloves for oral prep)
Verify baseline ECG (QTc must be within normal limits)
Check for drug interactions: Avoid PPIs and H2 antagonists (they severely decrease absorption). Stagger antacids by 2 hours.
Verify baseline CBC, LFTs, and electrolytes

manage_at_bedside: Mild nausea or diarrhea (manage with PRN meds per protocol)
Mild, non-debilitating muscle or joint aches
report_to_physician: Shortness of breath, dry cough, or chest pain
Rapid weight gain > 1-2 kg/day or severe peripheral edema
Unusual bleeding or bruising (e.g., melena, hematuria)

extravasation_management.action: Ensure tablets are swallowed whole with a meal and water. Do not crush, chew, or break.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Strict lab monitoring at 6-8 hours and 24 hours post-dose during the ramp-up phase.
stop_criteria: Any lab evidence of Tumor Lysis Syndrome (e.g., hyperkalemia, hyperuricemia)
Severe neutropenic fever

nursing_assessment: Monitor strict intake and output (I/O) to ensure adequate flushing of kidneys.
Assess for signs of TLS (lethargy, muscle cramps, decreased urine output).
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Take exactly at the same time every day with a meal. Drink at least 6-8 glasses of water daily. Avoid grapefruit, starfruit, and Seville oranges entirely.

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: Extreme risk of fatal Tumor Lysis Syndrome (TLS), especially during the 5-week dose ramp-up phase.
mandatory_checks: Verify baseline chemistry (K+, Uric Acid, Phosphorus, Calcium) prior to first dose and every dose escalation.
Confirm aggressive hydration (IV or PO target >1.5-2L/day) is active.
Verify prophylactic Allopurinol or Rasburicase is administered.
Confirm hazardous drug handling precautions (wear chemo gloves).

report_to_physician: Decreased urine output or dark urine
Muscle cramps, twitching, or irregular heartbeat (TLS alert)

extravasation_management.action: Swallow capsules whole with water. Do not open or crush under any circumstances.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Routine outpatient/inpatient monitoring. Regular CBC checks for myelosuppression.
stop_criteria: Clinical signs of DVT/PE (sudden shortness of breath, unilateral calf swelling/pain)
Development of blistering skin rash (Stevens-Johnson Syndrome risk)

nursing_assessment: Assess calves for tenderness, swelling, or erythema (DVT watch).
Monitor respiratory status for sudden dyspnea or hypoxia (PE watch).
Assess skin daily for new or worsening rashes.
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Strict adherence to birth control is mandatory. Do not donate blood or sperm. Immediately report any sudden leg swelling or chest pain.

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: Severe teratogen (fetal anomalies/death). High risk of Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism (PE).
mandatory_checks: Verify active REMS (RevAssist/Revlimid REMS) authorization and negative pregnancy test for females of childbearing potential.
Confirm active VTE prophylaxis is prescribed (e.g., Aspirin, Enoxaparin, or DOAC).
Confirm hazardous drug handling precautions (wear chemo gloves, especially if female nurse of childbearing age).

manage_at_bedside: Mild diarrhea or constipation (manage per bowel protocol)
report_to_physician: Unilateral leg swelling, warmth, or pain
Sudden chest pain or shortness of breath
New or spreading skin rash or mucosal lesions

extravasation_management.action: Administer with a meal and a large glass of water to minimize severe GI irritation.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Routine monitoring of daily weights, fluid status, and LFTs.
stop_criteria: Rapid, unexplained weight gain or severe respiratory distress
Severe elevation in liver transaminases or bilirubin
Severe, uncontrolled gastrointestinal hemorrhage

nursing_assessment: Monitor daily weights strictly (watch for >2 kg gain in a week).
Assess for periorbital edema, lower extremity edema, and auscultate lung bases.
Assess for signs of GI bleeding or severe nausea.
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Always take with food and a large glass of water. Report sudden weight gain, puffy eyes, or shortness of breath immediately. Avoid grapefruit juice.

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: Risk of severe fluid retention (pleural effusion, ascites, pulmonary edema) and hepatotoxicity.
mandatory_checks: Verify baseline Liver Function Tests (LFTs) and CBC.
Assess baseline weight and presence of peripheral edema.
Confirm hazardous drug handling precautions.
premeds: None routinely required, but anti-emetics may be needed if history of severe GI upset.

manage_at_bedside: Mild to moderate nausea (encourage taking with largest meal of the day)
Mild muscle cramps (manage with PRN analgesics/electrolytes if ordered)
report_to_physician: Rapid weight gain or sudden swelling around the eyes/legs
Shortness of breath or dyspnea on exertion
Jaundice (yellowing of eyes/skin) or dark urine
Black, tarry stools or vomiting blood

extravasation_management.action: Ensure tablets are swallowed whole with food to minimize GI irritation. Do not crush, cut, or chew.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Routine outpatient/inpatient lab monitoring (CBC, LFTs, electrolytes) heavily focused on hydration status.
stop_criteria: Grade 3 or 4 diarrhea (more than 7 stools per day over baseline) unresponsive to antidiarrheals
Severe elevation in liver transaminases

nursing_assessment: Strictly monitor bowel movement frequency and consistency.
Assess hydration status and electrolytes, especially if diarrhea is present.
Monitor daily weights for fluid retention (less common than with Dasatinib/Imatinib, but possible).
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Take with a meal. Do not eat grapefruit. Report severe diarrhea, yellowing of eyes/skin, or sudden weight gain immediately.

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: High risk of severe gastrointestinal toxicity (profuse diarrhea), hepatotoxicity, and myelosuppression.
mandatory_checks: Verify baseline Liver Function Tests (LFTs) and CBC.
Confirm hazardous drug handling precautions.
Check for drug interactions: Avoid PPIs (decreases absorption). If antacids or H2 blockers are needed, stagger by at least 2 hours.
premeds: Anti-diarrheals (e.g., Loperamide) should be readily available or ordered PRN.

manage_at_bedside: Mild to moderate diarrhea (initiate ordered PRN antidiarrheal protocol)
report_to_physician: Uncontrolled diarrhea despite Loperamide
Jaundice or dark-colored urine

extravasation_management.action: Swallow capsules whole with water. Do not break, chew, or open capsules.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Routine monitoring for VTE symptoms and myelosuppression (especially neutropenia).
stop_criteria: Clinical signs of DVT or PE
Development of blistering skin rash or angioedema

nursing_assessment: Assess calves for redness, swelling, and pain (DVT).
Monitor respiratory rate and oxygen saturation (PE).
Monitor CBC for profound neutropenia.
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Strict adherence to dual birth control methods. Do not donate blood or sperm. Report any leg pain, swelling, or sudden chest pain immediately.

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: Severe teratogen (fetal death/malformation). Extreme risk for Venous Thromboembolism (DVT/PE).
mandatory_checks: Verify active REMS (Pomalyst REMS) authorization and confirmed negative pregnancy tests.
Confirm concurrent VTE prophylaxis is prescribed (Aspirin, LMWH, or Warfarin) based on patient risk.
Confirm hazardous drug handling precautions (females of childbearing age should avoid handling if possible, or use strict PPE).
premeds: VTE prophylaxis (Anticoagulant/Antiplatelet) must be given as scheduled.

manage_at_bedside: Mild constipation (encourage fluids/fiber or PRN laxative)
report_to_physician: Unilateral leg swelling, calf pain, or warmth
Sudden shortness of breath or chest pain
Severe dizziness or confusion

extravasation_management.action: Administer at bedtime to minimize impact of severe somnolence/sedation. Swallow whole.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Continuous assessment of neurological baseline (sedation level and neuropathy).
stop_criteria: Grade 2 or higher peripheral neuropathy (painful tingling/loss of sensation)
Clinical signs of DVT/PE
Severe bradycardia or syncope

nursing_assessment: Assess daily for new numbness, tingling, or burning in hands/feet.
Monitor fall risk due to extreme sedation/somnolence and orthostatic hypotension.
Assess for signs of venous thromboembolism.
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Take at bedtime. Do not drive or operate machinery until you know how this affects you. Report numbness in fingers/toes immediately.

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: Severe teratogen (Phocomelia). High risk for severe, irreversible peripheral neuropathy, severe sedation, and DVT/PE.
mandatory_checks: Verify active REMS (Thalomid REMS) authorization and negative pregnancy tests.
Perform thorough baseline peripheral neuropathy assessment.
Confirm active VTE prophylaxis is prescribed.

manage_at_bedside: Somnolence (institute fall precautions, assist with ambulation)
Constipation (highly common; initiate aggressive bowel regimen)
report_to_physician: New or worsening numbness, burning, or tingling in extremities
Leg swelling or chest pain (VTE)
Severe blistering skin rash
Syncope or severe dizziness

extravasation_management.action: Administer with a glass of water at the same time each day. Do not open, break, or chew capsules/tablets.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Routine BP checks and cardiac rhythm monitoring if hospitalized.
stop_criteria: Major hemorrhage (GI bleed, intracranial hemorrhage)
New onset irregular pulse or palpitations (suspected Atrial Fibrillation)
Uncontrolled hypertension

nursing_assessment: Assess strictly for signs of bleeding (petechiae, melena, hematuria, excessive bruising).
Monitor apical pulse for irregularity.
Monitor blood pressure for new or worsening hypertension.
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Avoid grapefruit and Seville oranges. Stop taking and notify doctor before any surgery or dental work. Report heart racing, dizziness, or any unusual bleeding.

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: High risk of severe/fatal hemorrhage (especially if on concurrent blood thinners) and sudden cardiac arrhythmias (Atrial Fibrillation).
mandatory_checks: Verify baseline ECG and recent blood pressure trends.
Check current medication list for concurrent anticoagulants or antiplatelets (major bleeding risk).
Confirm drug is held 3-7 days prior to any planned surgical procedures.

manage_at_bedside: Transient increase in ALC (lymphocytosis is an expected pharmacodynamic effect, not necessarily progression)
report_to_physician: Palpitations, racing heart, or feeling of 'fluttering' in chest
Any signs of bleeding (blood in stool/urine, severe nosebleeds)
Severe headache or sudden neurological changes (intracranial bleed risk)

extravasation_management.action: Administer consistently with or without food. AVOID taking with milk or dairy products (contains xanthine oxidase, which degrades the drug).

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Routine CBC and liver enzyme checks.
stop_criteria: Severe myelosuppression (ANC < 500 or Platelets < 50k)
Severe hepatotoxicity (jaundice, significantly elevated AST/ALT)

nursing_assessment: Monitor strictly for signs of infection or bleeding.
Assess for jaundice or right upper quadrant pain.
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Take exactly as prescribed. Do not take with milk. Wash hands after handling. Report fever or easy bruising.

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: Severe, potentially fatal myelosuppression. Lethal drug interaction with Allopurinol or Febuxostat.
mandatory_checks: CRITICAL: Check MAR for Allopurinol. If concurrent, the 6-MP dose MUST be drastically reduced (typically by 75%) to avoid fatal toxicity.
Verify pharmacogenomic testing results if available (TPMT or NUDT15 deficiency requires massive dose reduction).
Verify baseline CBC and LFTs.

manage_at_bedside: Mild nausea or loss of appetite
report_to_physician: Fever > 38.0°C (100.4°F) or chills
Yellowing of skin or eyes
Unusual bleeding or pinpoint red spots on skin (petechiae)

extravasation_management.action: Administer WITH FOOD to enhance absorption and mitigate severe nausea. Swallow whole.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Continuous ECG monitoring if inpatient or frequent outpatient checks. Respiratory assessments.
stop_criteria: Acute onset of unexplained dyspnea, hypoxia, or infiltrates on CXR (suspect ILD)
QTc prolongation > 500 msec

nursing_assessment: Auscultate lungs regularly and monitor oxygen saturation.
Assess efficacy of anti-emetic regimen.
Monitor daily ECGs if concurrently given with other QTc prolonging drugs (e.g., Ondansetron, Fluconazole).
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Always take with food to prevent severe nausea. Do not crush. Report any sudden breathing difficulty or feeling of a racing heartbeat.

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: Risk of severe pulmonary toxicity (Interstitial Lung Disease/Pneumonitis) and prolonged QTc interval.
mandatory_checks: Verify baseline ECG (ensure QTc < 450-470 msec depending on protocol).
Check baseline chest x-ray and respiratory status.
Confirm aggressive prophylactic anti-emetics are ordered (highly emetogenic for an oral drug).
premeds: 5-HT3 Antagonist (e.g., Ondansetron) 30-60 mins prior to administration.

manage_at_bedside: Nausea and vomiting (manage aggressively with ordered anti-emetics)
report_to_physician: New or worsening shortness of breath or persistent dry cough
Palpitations, irregular heartbeat, or dizziness

extravasation_management.action: Administer with or without food. Swallow whole with a cup of water.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Strict daily monitoring of weight, respiratory status, and neuro status (for PRES) in the first month.
stop_criteria: Unexplained respiratory distress, hypoxia, or pleural effusions (Differentiation Syndrome)
Seizures, altered mental status, or sudden severe headache (PRES)
QTc prolongation > 500 msec

nursing_assessment: Monitor strictly for signs of Differentiation Syndrome (fever, dyspnea, rapid weight gain, edema).
Perform routine neuro-checks for signs of PRES.
Monitor serial ECGs per protocol.
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Report sudden weight gain, trouble breathing, or severe headache immediately. Avoid grapefruit juice.

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: High risk of Differentiation Syndrome (can be fatal), QTc prolongation, and Posterior Reversible Encephalopathy Syndrome (PRES).
mandatory_checks: Verify baseline ECG and electrolytes (correct Potassium and Magnesium before dosing).
Assess baseline respiratory and neurological status.
Confirm hazardous drug handling precautions.
premeds: Prophylactic corticosteroids may be ordered if WBC is rapidly rising (Differentiation Syndrome prophylaxis).

report_to_physician: Shortness of breath, unexplained fever, or rapid weight gain (Differentiation alert)
Sudden severe headache, vision changes, or confusion (PRES alert)
Palpitations or fainting

extravasation_management.action: STOP IMMEDIATELY. Do not flush or irrigate. Aspirate residual drug. Apply COLD PACKS.
extravasation_management.antidote: Dexrazoxane (Totect®) IV infusion or topical DMSO per protocol.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Continuous direct nurse observation during IV push. Check line patency and blood return every 2-5 mL.
stop_criteria: ANY loss of blood return, resistance, or patient report of burning/stinging
Acute cardiac arrhythmias on telemetry

nursing_assessment: Assess oral cavity daily for severe mucositis and ulcerations.
Monitor total cumulative lifetime dose tracking closely to avoid cardiotoxicity thresholds.
Monitor CBC for severe nadir (typically days 10-14).
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Urine will turn a red-orange color for 24-48 hours; this is harmless. Report any pain or redness at the injection site immediately.

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: CRITICAL VESICANT. Can cause severe tissue necrosis, sloughing, and functional loss. Dose-dependent lifetime cumulative cardiotoxicity leading to irreversible CHF.
mandatory_checks: Verify central line patency with brisk, flawless blood return prior to starting.
Confirm baseline ECHO or MUGA scan shows acceptable Left Ventricular Ejection Fraction (LVEF).
Double-check lifetime cumulative dose limits before administration.
premeds: Aggressive anti-emetics (High emetic risk: 5-HT3 antagonist + Dexamethasone + NK1 antagonist).

manage_at_bedside: Reassure patient regarding red/orange urine discoloration.
Provide ice chips (cryotherapy) during infusion to reduce mucositis severity.
report_to_physician: Signs of extravasation (pain, swelling, or redness at the site).
Signs of acute congestive heart failure (sudden dyspnea, peripheral edema, rapid weight gain).

extravasation_management.action: Monitor IV site for local patency. Manage per standard non-vesicant protocol.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Vitals every 15 minutes during titration for the first hour, then every 30-60 minutes.
stop_criteria: Severe infusion-related reaction (bronchospasm, angioedema, hypotension, or throat tightness)
Rigors or shaking chills uncontrolled by bedside interventions

nursing_assessment: Monitor for delayed hypersensitivity reactions up to 24 hours post-infusion.
Assess for signs of Tumor Lysis Syndrome (TLS) in patients with high tumor burdens.
Monitor blood pressure trends post-infusion.
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Report any sudden shortness of breath, rash, hives, or swelling of the lips/throat that occurs after leaving the unit.

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: Severe, potentially fatal Infusion-Related Reactions (IRR), typically occurring within 30-120 minutes of the first dose. High risk of Hepatitis B reactivation.
mandatory_checks: Verify negative Hepatitis B core antibody and surface antigen screens before the first cycle.
Ensure emergency anaphylaxis kit and oxygen setup are at the bedside.
Confirm all ordered premedications were completed exactly 30-60 minutes prior to spiking the bag.

manage_at_bedside: Mild rigors (administer ordered PRN IV meperidine strictly per protocol).
Mild flushing or pruritus (slow infusion rate and monitor closely).
report_to_physician: Hypotension, wheezing, or stridor (signs of anaphylaxis/severe IRR).
Chest pain or palpitations.

extravasation_management.action: Stop infusion immediately. Aspirate. Apply WARM compresses to aid dissipation.
extravasation_management.antidote: Hyaluronidase (if institutional protocol permits for large volume irritant/vesicant crossovers).

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Check blood pressure every 15 minutes for the first hour of infusion.
stop_criteria: Sudden precipitate drop in systolic blood pressure
Anaphylaxis, wheezing, or profound facial flushing

nursing_assessment: Assess for orthostatic changes before ambulating the patient post-infusion.
Monitor CBC strictly (severe bone marrow suppression risk, especially leukopenia).
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Rise slowly from a sitting or lying position. Report any fevers, chills, or unusual bruising immediately.

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: Can cause fatal, acute hypotension if infused too rapidly (must be administered over at least 30-60 minutes). Severe anaphylactic-like reactions risk.
mandatory_checks: Verify that the infusion duration is set for at least 60 minutes minimum on the pump.
Inspect the bag closely for precipitation (Etoposide is highly concentration-dependent and unstable if mixed incorrectly).
Check baseline blood pressure prior to spiking the line.

manage_at_bedside: Mild transient hypotension (slow the pump down further if ordered parameter allows).
report_to_physician: Severe, symptomatic hypotension or syncope.
Signs of bronchospasm or respiratory distress.
